title: SD 5-Foa Plates For Yeast Counter Selection
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"anfme","text":"This protocol is for the prepartation of SD 5-Foa plates to use for the counter selection of URA3 in yeast.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"rj64","text":"Integrated marker cassette of ‘loxp-kanMX-URA-loxp’ is proven to be removed by the low-occurrence event of mitotic recombination between the repeated loxp-loxp, and positive colonies could be selected out by marker loss upon URA counter-selection in a one-step process.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"57ml7","text":"6.7g/L Yeast Nitrogen Base (No amino acids, no amonium sulfate)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":6,"length":57}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8lu56","text":"5g/L Ammonium Sulfate","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":5,"length":16}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bmig0","text":"2% (w/v) Glucose","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":9,"length":7}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ntg2","text":"2% (w/v) Agar","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":9,"length":4}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7io6","text":"1g/L  5-Fluoroorotic Acid","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":6,"length":19}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6p5cd","text":"50mg/L Uracil","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":7,"length":6}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"75j59","text":"(According to Manfucaturers specifications) -Ura Drop out Mix","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":44,"length":17}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: In a clean bottle, add Yeast Nitrogen Base (No amino acids, no amonium sulfate), Ammonium Sulfate and glucose.
In another clean bottle, add Agar.
Add DI H2O to both bottles. (If preparing for 100mL, suggest to add 60mL to bottle containg Agar , and the rest of the 40mL to the other bottle)
Once cooled to around 50 o C, add 5-Fluoroorotic Acid, Uracil, and -Ura Drop out Mix to the bottle containing Yeast Nitrogen Base (No amino acids, no amonium sulfate), Ammonium Sulfate and glucose.
Filter Sterile using 0.22um filter of contents in above bottle, into bottle containing Agar.
Dispense agar medium into petri dish (Recommend around ~15mL per petri dish)
Innoculate transformed yeast cells from YPD agar plates (or whatever medium used) into 5mL of YPD. Remember to add markers in 5mL YPD.
Let it grow overnight in 30 o C incubator, with shaking of 200rpm.
Centrifuge overnight culture at 1,100 xG for 5minutes.
Remove supernatant and wash cells with 5mL of sterile DI water.
After final washing step, resuspend cells in 1mL of DI water
Plate 150 - 200uL of cells onto SD 5-Foa agar plates. (try not to plate too much cells onto agar plate, if not it will be overcrowded and cells will not grow)
If counter selection is successful, colonies should appear after 2-3 days.
Inoculate colonies from SD 5Foa plate into 5mL YPD medium.
Let it grow overnight in 30 o C incubator, with shaking of 200rpm.
Plate 100 - 150uL of cells onto YPD agar with markers.
If counter selection and marker excision is successful, there should not be any colonies seen on the plates.
creator: Ryan Tan
